title: Next validation source after the expanded live library
goal: Prefer real memloft corpus validation over continuing to grow synthetic scenarios once the larger library is green.

records.text: After the expanded live-library reached 66/66 in exact and ann mode, the next highest-value femind validation step is testing against sampled real memloft data rather than continuing to grow synthetic real-world scenarios first. Additional scenario expansion can still help later, but real corpus validation should come next.

The femind live-library corpus was expanded from 12 scenarios / 44 checks to 18 scenarios / 66 checks. After targeted tuning of strict grounding for numeric/time-unit detail queries, combined-capability yes/no negatives, and evaluator synonym handling, both all+exact and all+ann now pass 66/66 on the expanded corpus.

The larger femind real-world library now passes 44/44 in both all+exact and all+ann mode. The key fix was separating contradicted yes/no questions from true abstentions and widening strict grounding for unsupported ordinal and removal or failure detail queries.
The key fix was not expanding the corpus first. The key fix was separating contradicted yes or no questions from true abstentions and widening strict grounding.

retrieval_checks.query: What should femind validate next after the expanded live library reached 66 out of 66?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The next validation step should use sampled real memloft data.

retrieval_checks.query: What is the current expanded live-library size?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The current expanded live-library size is 18 scenarios and 66 checks.

retrieval_checks.query: Did ANN match exact on the expanded live library?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Yes, both all+exact and all+ann pass 66 out of 66.

retrieval_checks.query: Was the key fix for the larger library expanding the corpus first?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No, the main fix was separating contradicted yes or no questions from true abstentions and tightening strict grounding.

abstention_checks.query: What exact wall-clock duration did the 66 out of 66 ANN run take?

title: Extraction defaults and backend lanes
goal: Retrieve the locked defaults and backend separation from real memloft decisions.

records.text: Locked femind extraction defaults after practical and live-usage comparison. API default is openai/gpt-oss-120b. CLI default is gpt-5.4-mini. Lower-cost CLI fallback is gpt-5.1-codex-mini.

For femind extraction comparison, add gpt-5.1-codex-mini alongside gpt-5.4-mini so the CLI lane compares two small models. Compare those two CLI models against two smaller API models using the same extraction scenarios and summary format.
Added a selectable extraction backend to the femind practical_eval runner. The standard API path remains the default, while codex-cli now supports extraction smoke testing with gpt-5.4-mini.

retrieval_checks.query: What is the locked API extraction default for femind?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The locked API extraction default is openai/gpt-oss-120b.

retrieval_checks.query: What is the locked CLI extraction default?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The locked CLI extraction default is gpt-5.4-mini.

retrieval_checks.query: What lower-cost CLI fallback is kept in the comparison lane?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The lower-cost CLI fallback is gpt-5.1-codex-mini.

retrieval_checks.query: Does the practical eval runner support a codex-cli extraction backend?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Yes, the practical eval runner supports a codex-cli extraction backend.

abstention_checks.query: Which Azure OpenAI deployment name is the default CLI extraction backend?

title: Standard practical path and ANN rollout
goal: Verify the standard maintainer path, exact baseline, and ANN rollout policy from real project memory.

records.text: The standard local live-validation entry point for femind is scripts/run-practical-eval.sh, which defaults to retrieval mode with exact vector search and writes target/practical-eval/retrieval-exact.json.
After adding yes/no supersession-aware matching to the practical evaluator, the typed practical retrieval-only exact-mode run now passes 9/9 checks.
Add ANN as an optional, feature-gated component for production use, but keep brute-force enabled for current benchmarks.

retrieval_checks.query: What script is the standard local practical validation entry point for femind?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The standard local practical validation entry point is scripts/run-practical-eval.sh.

retrieval_checks.query: What does scripts/run-practical-eval.sh default to for vector search?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: scripts/run-practical-eval.sh defaults to exact vector search.

retrieval_checks.query: How did the typed practical exact run perform?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The typed practical exact run passes 9 out of 9 checks.

retrieval_checks.query: Is ANN mandatory in every femind build?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No, ANN is optional and feature-gated.

abstention_checks.query: What exact HNSW M value is the practical runner configured to use by default?

title: Grounding, scoring, and abstention policy
goal: Stress the rules that keep exact-mode retrieval from over-answering unsupported details.

records.text: For exact/detail-seeking queries in semantic retrieval, femind now requires lexical grounding beyond semantic similarity before returning results. This prevents exact-mode retrieval from surfacing semantically related but unsupported memories for missing-answer questions like exact token cost.
femind now defaults to a composite post-search scorer combining recency, importance, and memory type instead of raw retrieval scores only.
Include 30 no-answer abstention questions in the test suite to ensure retrieval does not surface misleading context for questions femind should not answer.

retrieval_checks.query: What does exact-mode semantic retrieval require for detail-seeking queries?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Exact-mode semantic retrieval requires lexical grounding beyond semantic similarity.

retrieval_checks.query: Why was lexical grounding added to exact-mode retrieval?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Lexical grounding was added to stop unsupported exact-detail answers from semantically related memories.

retrieval_checks.query: What signals are combined in the default post-search scorer?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The default post-search scorer combines recency, importance, and memory type.

retrieval_checks.query: How many no-answer abstention questions should be in the test suite?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The test suite should include 30 no-answer abstention questions.

abstention_checks.query: What exact token count threshold triggers the lexical-grounding filter?

title: Cognitive memory model and validation priority
goal: Verify that the optimized memory model and real-world-first validation order survive in the real memloft corpus.

records.text: femind uses three cognitive memory types as the core optimized memory model: episodic for events and sessions, semantic for facts and preferences, and procedural for workflows, error patterns, and solutions.

For femind, prioritize real-world accuracy validation over benchmark chasing. Use benchmark datasets only when they materially help validate real retrieval or extraction behavior or provide regression coverage.

retrieval_checks.query: What are the three cognitive memory types in femind?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The three cognitive memory types are episodic, semantic, and procedural.

retrieval_checks.query: What kind of knowledge belongs in procedural memory?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Procedural memory holds workflows, error patterns, and solutions.

retrieval_checks.query: Should femind optimize for benchmark scores before real-world confidence?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No, femind should prioritize real-world accuracy validation before benchmark chasing.

retrieval_checks.query: When are benchmark datasets useful for femind?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Benchmark datasets are useful when they materially validate real retrieval or extraction behavior or provide regression coverage.

abstention_checks.query: What emotional-memory subtype was added as the fourth cognitive memory type?

title: Production ingest versus benchmark chunking
goal: Separate production extraction behavior from benchmark-only chunking behavior.

records.text: Do not both log each memory and chunk it; select one approach. In production, use LLM extraction to generate individual fact memories with graph edges and no chunking. For benchmarks lacking LLM extraction, use raw-text chunking to create chunk memories with no graph.
In production memloft processes each incoming message, the LLM extracts individual facts which become separate memory records; no chunking is performed.

retrieval_checks.query: How does production memloft ingest new information?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Production memloft uses LLM extraction to turn incoming messages into individual fact memories.

retrieval_checks.query: Does production memloft chunk incoming messages?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No, production memloft does not chunk incoming messages.

retrieval_checks.query: When should raw-text chunking be used instead of extraction?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Raw-text chunking should be used for benchmarks when LLM extraction is unavailable.

retrieval_checks.query: Should the system both log each memory and chunk it?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No, the system should select one approach instead of doing both.

abstention_checks.query: Which exact graph-edge labels are generated for every extracted production fact?

title: Benchmark outcomes, gaps, and successor harness
goal: Keep benchmark knowledge accurate without letting it override current product behavior.

records.text: Benchmarks validated the core search pipeline, tuned embedding and ranking parameters, built the retrieval test harness, and created the embedding infrastructure with local and API backends and caching.
Benchmarks did not test LLM fact extraction quality, graph-based retrieval, or real conversation memory with incremental processing of conversations.
RecallBench is the successor benchmark harness for femind and other memory systems. The old in-repo femind-bench should be treated as obsolete except for historical benchmark artifacts.

retrieval_checks.query: What did the earlier benchmarks validate well?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The earlier benchmarks validated the core search pipeline, embedding and ranking tuning, the retrieval harness, and the embedding infrastructure.

retrieval_checks.query: What important things did the benchmarks not test?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The benchmarks did not test LLM fact extraction quality, graph-based retrieval, or real conversation memory.

retrieval_checks.query: What replaced the old in-repo femind-bench?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: RecallBench replaced the old in-repo femind-bench.

retrieval_checks.query: Should benchmark history still matter at all?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Yes, benchmark history still matters as historical validation of the core search pipeline.

abstention_checks.query: What exact RecallBench accuracy score was achieved on the Oracle variant in this memory slice?

title: Embedding defaults and rationale
goal: Retrieve the real embedding choice and why it won across backends.

records.text: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 is the best local model for memloft: fastest, already in use, and sufficiently accurate.

Use all-MiniLM-L6-v2 consistently across mindcore embedding backends because smaller embedding models achieve about ninety percent of larger-model quality while reducing compute. Search pipeline quality, chunking strategy, context assembly, and reranking matter more for retrieval accuracy.
Use all-MiniLM-L6-v2 with 384-dimensional vectors consistently across the local Candle backend, API backend, and API-with-local-fallback wrapper.

retrieval_checks.query: What embedding model is preferred for memloft and femind?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The preferred embedding model is all-MiniLM-L6-v2.

retrieval_checks.query: Why was all-MiniLM-L6-v2 preferred?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: It was preferred because it is fast, already in use, sufficiently accurate, and smaller models reduce compute.

retrieval_checks.query: What vector dimensionality is used with all-MiniLM-L6-v2 in this stack?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 uses 384-dimensional vectors.

retrieval_checks.query: What matters more than jumping to a larger embedding model?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Search pipeline quality, chunking strategy, context assembly, and reranking matter more than a larger embedding model.

abstention_checks.query: What exact MTEB retrieval score was recorded for all-MiniLM-L6-v2 in this slice?

title: Benchmark cache architecture and result history
goal: Test exact detail retrieval for cache design without inventing unsupported file or hash details.

records.text: Pre-embedding cache architecture for benchmark test vectors is implemented in RecallBench, not in femind core. It pre-loads and caches benchmark vectors instead of re-embedding per question, reducing a 500-question run from roughly thirty minutes to about thirty seconds.

The RecallBench cache database contains two tables: session_chunks with session_id, chunk_index, chunk_text, embedding blob; and cache_meta with model_name, chunk_size, overlap, dataset_hash, and created_at.
Preserve benchmark results with version numbering such as v1 and v2 instead of deleting previous runs so maintainers can compare regressions and improvements over time.

retrieval_checks.query: Where is the benchmark embedding cache architecture implemented?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The benchmark embedding cache architecture is implemented in RecallBench, not in femind core.

retrieval_checks.query: What are the two main tables in the RecallBench cache database?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The two main tables are session_chunks and cache_meta.

retrieval_checks.query: Why should benchmark results be kept with version numbers like v1 and v2?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Benchmark results should be kept with version numbers so maintainers can compare regressions and improvements over time.

retrieval_checks.query: What kind of speedup was the benchmark cache designed to provide?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The benchmark cache was designed to cut a 500-question run from roughly thirty minutes to about thirty seconds.

abstention_checks.query: What exact dataset hash value is stored in cache_meta for the current benchmark corpus?

title: Rename sequence and release gating
goal: Verify current identity versus stale rename-era guidance and keep release steps grounded.

records.text: For mindcore, complete ANN runtime mode support and non-LLM verification before renaming the crate and repo to femind. Live LLM-backed validation remained blocked pending explicit user approval.

mindcore was relocated into ~/projects/fe-suite/fe-mind and the local crate and package were renamed to femind. The authoritative local spec database is ~/projects/fe-suite/fe-mind/.docs/femind_spec.db.

retrieval_checks.query: What were the local crate and package renamed to?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The local crate and package were renamed to femind.

title: Documentation source of truth
goal: Test retrieval of the suite documentation architecture from real memloft decisions.

records.text: fe-suite documentation should use SQLite as the primary source of truth, with markdown and HTML generated on demand and only committed as human-readable docs when required for GitHub or external presentation.
Additional documentation context is also captured in git commit messages, memloft failure-analysis logs, and spec files, but the primary source of truth remains the SQLite documentation databases.

retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The primary documentation source of truth for the fe-suite is SQLite documentation databases.

retrieval_checks.query: Should markdown be the primary documentation source of truth in the suite?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No, markdown should not be the primary documentation source of truth in the suite.

retrieval_checks.query: When should human-readable markdown or HTML docs be committed?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Human-readable markdown or HTML docs should be committed when they are required for GitHub or external presentation.

retrieval_checks.query: Do commit messages and failure-analysis logs replace the SQLite doc databases as the main source of truth?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No, commit messages and failure-analysis logs do not replace the SQLite doc databases as the main source of truth.

title: Current MCP transport versus outdated descriptions
goal: Verify current-state retrieval when older or outdated transport descriptions exist elsewhere.

records.text: Codex memloft failures were caused by Codex launching memloftd mcp-stdio instead of using the daemon HTTP MCP endpoint, and by memloftd having stateful_mode disabled so initialize no longer returned Mcp-Session-Id. The fix switched Codex to http://127.0.0.1:18820/mcp and restored stateful MCP behavior.

The active memloft MCP contract is daemon-hosted HTTP at http://127.0.0.1:18820/mcp with stateful sessions enabled. Docs describing memloft-mcp stdio transport or stateless MCP are outdated unless clearly marked historical.

retrieval_checks.query: What is the active memloft MCP endpoint now?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The active memloft MCP endpoint is http://127.0.0.1:18820/mcp.

retrieval_checks.query: Does the active memloft MCP contract use stateful sessions?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Yes, the active memloft MCP contract uses stateful sessions.

retrieval_checks.query: Should docs that describe memloft-mcp stdio transport still be treated as current?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No, docs describing memloft-mcp stdio transport should be treated as outdated unless marked historical.

retrieval_checks.query: What two issues caused Codex memloft transport failures?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The failures were caused by launching mcp-stdio instead of the HTTP MCP endpoint and by stateful_mode being disabled.

abstention_checks.query: What exact request header value was sent in the failing initialize call before the transport fix?

title: Vault migration fix and release policy
goal: Exercise exact retrieval around current maintenance state without inventing unsupported distribution details.

records.text: The vault startup warning was caused by legacy secrets still stored as SQLite TEXT in ~/.memloft/vault.db. Fixed vault.rs to migrate both TEXT and BLOB rows, backed up vault.db, converted the four remaining TEXT rows to BLOB, and verified startup now logs a successful migration instead of a warning.

Memloft should stay in local tracked-development mode for now. Maintain a developer-style CHANGELOG.md, but do not create GitHub releases or commit-triggered CI workflows until distribution is actually needed.

retrieval_checks.query: What caused the memloft vault startup warning?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The memloft vault startup warning was caused by legacy secrets still stored as SQLite TEXT in vault.db.

retrieval_checks.query: What was done to fix the vault warning?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: The fix migrated both TEXT and BLOB rows, backed up vault.db, and converted the four remaining TEXT rows to BLOB.

retrieval_checks.query: Should memloft create GitHub releases right now by default?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No, memloft should stay in local tracked-development mode for now.

retrieval_checks.query: Should commit-triggered CI workflows be kept active for memloft right now?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No, commit-triggered CI workflows should not be kept active for memloft right now.

abstention_checks.query: What exact encryption key material was used during the vault row migration?

The femind live-library corpus was expanded from 12 scenarios / 44 checks to 18 scenarios / 66 checks and now passes 66/66 in both all+exact and all+ann mode.

title: What comes after femind shipping
goal: Keep suite sequencing grounded in the real memloft decisions.

records.text: Finish shipping the femind crate before starting work on the feloop suite.
Before starting the next suite target, run cargo publish --dry-run, perform a metadata and package audit, and clean up the changelog and release notes for femind.

title: What benchmark history can and cannot prove
goal: Make sure benchmark success does not get overstated into unsupported claims about extraction and real conversation memory.

records.text: Benchmarks validated the core search pipeline, tuned embedding and ranking parameters, built the retrieval test harness, and created the embedding infrastructure.
Benchmarks did not test LLM fact extraction quality, graph-based retrieval, or real conversation memory.

Sonnet is the harder test case for validating retrieval quality. If the retrieval pipeline surfaces correct context for Sonnet, higher-tier models should perform at least as well on the same retrieved context.

retrieval_checks.query: What did benchmark work prove reliably?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Benchmark work reliably proved the core search pipeline, embedding and ranking tuning, and retrieval harness.

retrieval_checks.query: What did benchmark work not prove reliably?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Benchmark work did not prove LLM fact extraction quality, graph-based retrieval, or real conversation memory.

retrieval_checks.query: Why was Sonnet treated as the harder validation case?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: Sonnet was treated as the harder validation case because if retrieval surfaces the right context for Sonnet, higher-tier models should do at least as well.

retrieval_checks.query: Can benchmark success alone prove femind is ready for real conversation memory extraction?
retrieval_checks.expected_answer: No, benchmark success alone cannot prove femind is ready for real conversation memory extraction.

abstention_checks.query: What exact benchmark score threshold was defined as the pass bar for Sonnet in this slice?
